title: Top-N Optimisation degrades performance for large LIMITs
description:
### What happens?

Hi dear DuckDB team,

While using DuckDB I found a regression concerning the `TOP-N` optimization if there are large limits.



### To Reproduce

```sql
CREATE TABLE A AS FROM range(10_000_000) order by hash(range);
.timer on
FROM A order by range LIMIT 5_000_000;
-- Run Time (s): real 3.937 user 4.248405 sys 0.212751
PRAGMA disabled_optimizers='top_n';
FROM A order by range LIMIT 5_000_000;
-- Run Time (s): real 0.133 user 0.709412 sys 0.180778` 
```
Note that it is both slower in real time and user time. I run it on today's duckdb nightly (v1.3.0-dev1011 d0c7224b40)

### OS:

iOS

### DuckDB Version:

nightly (05.03.2025)

### DuckDB Client:

Shell

### Hardware:

Macbook Pro M4

### Full Name:

Paul Gross

### Affiliation:

CWI

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a nightly build

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Yes

- [x] Yes, I have

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE B (id Int32, value String NOT NULL, created_at DateTime DEFAULT now());
INSERT INTO B (id, value) VALUES (1, 'test1'), (2, 'test2'), (3, NULL), (4, 'test4');
CREATE TABLE C AS SELECT id, value FROM B WHERE value IS NOT NULL ORDER BY id;
SELECT * FROM C ORDER BY created_at LIMIT 5000000;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM C WHERE value LIKE 'test%';
key_bug_features: TOP-N optimization performance degradation with large LIMITs
